log.text: A traveling salt-auditor named [char:Irret Sou] was berthed at [loc:quayside] when the body was found — she reached it before anyone else did, and she mentioned to the runner who summoned help that something about how the body lay had struck her as wrong.

The victim [char:Tessek Oravi] had been sent by the Matron to review ledgers at [loc:records_room] that same evening — the room was found unlocked the following morning, in violation of standing orders that [char:Nitha Tessek] herself enforces.

The night-watcher [char:Senne Arak] holds her post at [loc:upper_walkway], which overlooks the passage between the Matron's House and the salt-works — she would have been on watch during the hours in question.
Word has reached the investigator at [loc:quayside] — [char:Tessek Oravi] has been found at [loc:salt_works]. The investigation begins.
Flat-bottomed trade boats rock against salt-bleached pilings as the morning fog lifts in slow ribbons off the estuary water.

The investigator encounters [char:Irret Sou] at [loc:quayside]. A traveling salt-auditor from an inland trade council, a composed woman of thirty who has been berthed at the quayside for three days conducting a routine cargo review. She was on deck before dawn for the morning muster and was the first outsider to reach the body; she is forthcoming with what she observed and speaks with the precision of someone accustomed to recording exactly what she sees.

[char:Irret Sou] tells the investigator that when she first knelt beside the body, she noticed a thin dark line running along the left side of Tessek Oravi's robe — too straight and too narrow to be a fold or a shadow — which she noted before anyone else disturbed the scene.

[char:Irret Sou] tells the investigator that [time:earlier that same evening], [char:Tessek Oravi] had told her she intended to check a cargo manifest in the dockside ledger office before the night was out.

[char:Irret Sou] tells the investigator that when she first saw the body at sunrise, the color of the exposed skin still appeared to her as relatively fresh — she had assumed at the time that the death must have occurred very recently, perhaps within the hour.
Wide shallow pans stretch toward the estuary's edge, their surfaces crusted and still; the harvest is over and the flat-boats are beached, hull-up, along the near bank.

The investigator examines the body of [char:Tessek Oravi]. A meticulous woman of about fifty, lineage bookkeeper for the Tessek family and a respected salt-merchant in her own right. She is found slumped against the railing of the salt-pan platform, her posture too composed for a fall — and her outer robe, despite the heavy pre-dawn fog, is dry.

The investigator encounters [char:Maret Doun] at [loc:salt_works]. The Tessek salt-works overseer, a sturdy woman of about forty who speaks in the measured tones of someone used to giving direction. She is the one who found the body and sent the overnight runner for help, and she answers questions about it with a steadiness that sits just at the edge of composure.

The investigator finds [item:Salt-Scraper]. A narrow iron-bladed tool, roughly palm-length, used to lift caked salt from the harvest pans; the blade is clean and the handle wood is pale and unweathered, showing none of the grey salt-staining common to tools in regular use.
The investigator finds [item:Records Room Key]. A heavy iron key with a Tessek lineage mark filed into the bow; the shank shows no tarnish, and a faint damp smell clings to the metal.

Examining the body at [loc:salt_works], the investigator shifts the outer robe aside and finds a narrow puncture wound on the left side of Tessek Oravi's torso; the robe itself has a corresponding slit in the fabric, its edges stiff with dried dark brown residue.

At the edge of the salt-pan platform where the body was found, Tessek Oravi's hands carry a visible coating of wet salt residue, and the railing she is slumped against faces directly onto the nearest brine channel.

[char:Maret Doun] tells the investigator that she saw [char:Tessek Oravi] at [loc:salt_works] [time:in the late afternoon], and that Oravi had seemed agitated and distracted, speaking in clipped sentences and leaving before their conversation was finished.

When shown the key, [char:Maret Doun] states that she found it lying on the floor inside the [loc:records_room] doorway the morning after the death and had kept it intending to return it to the Matron; when the investigator asks when she entered the Records Room to find it, Maret pauses and then says she found it from the doorway, without going in.
A covered passage of salt-weathered planks runs between the Matron's House and the works complex, its boards damp and filmed with the fine white residue that settles everywhere in Vaur Tessek.

The investigator encounters [char:Senne Arak] at [loc:upper_walkway]. The Matron's night-watcher, a young woman of about twenty-five who stands at her post with the careful stillness of someone trying not to be noticed. She is quick to speak but her answers arrive in the slightly-too-complete way of someone who has rehearsed them.

The investigator finds [item:Watch-Log Tablet]. A wooden tablet carved with rows of notched marks recording check-in intervals; the notches on one side of a blank gap are pressed deeper into the wood than those on the other side.

[char:Senne Arak] tells the investigator that [time:during the deep part of the night], while she was on watch at [loc:upper_walkway], she heard a single heavy thud from the direction of the Matron's complex — loud enough to make her stop and listen, though she heard nothing after it.

[char:Senne Arak] tells the investigator that [time:in the middle of the night] she heard footsteps on the boards of [loc:upper_walkway] moving in the direction of the storage eaves, followed by a scraping sound she could not identify.

[char:Senne Arak] tells the investigator that she personally watched [char:Tessek Oravi] enter the Matron's complex [time:at the second bell of night], and that by [time:one bell before first light], when Senne's watch ended, Oravi had not come back out through the main entrance.

When the investigator examines the [loc:upper_walkway] watch-log tablet with [char:Senne Arak] present, she identifies a gap of roughly [time:forty minutes] around [time:the third bell of night] containing no notched entries; asked to explain it, Senne says she stepped inside briefly to warm her hands and lost track of the time.

[char:Senne Arak] tells the investigator that [time:some weeks before the death], she overheard [char:Tessek Oravi] in a sharp exchange with someone she could not see, in which Oravi said clearly: 'those records are not yours to take, and they never will be.'
Bolts of undyed canvas overhead filter the pale autumn light into something softer, and the air holds the mingled smell of brine, clay salt, and lamp oil.

The investigator encounters [char:Dauvek Pol] at [loc:covered_market]. Tessek Oravi's nephew — her sister's son — a broad-shouldered man of about thirty-five who trades at the covered market in display salts and cured goods. He does not hide that he had been pressing his aunt to have a lineage document revised in his favor, and he speaks of her with the brittle warmth of a man who has been recently refused.

[char:Dauvek Pol] tells the investigator that in the weeks before her death, [char:Tessek Oravi] had complained to him of persistent stomach pains and had twice visited the herbalist who keeps a stall at [loc:covered_market].

[char:Dauvek Pol] tells the investigator that [time:late in the evening], he saw [char:Tessek Oravi] walking in the direction of [loc:covered_market] with a ledger tucked under one arm, and she did not acknowledge him when he called out to her.

[char:Dauvek Pol] tells the investigator that he encountered [char:Tessek Oravi] [time:just after the evening meal] and that she was in good health — speaking clearly, moving quickly — which was the last time he saw her alive.

[char:Dauvek Pol] shows the investigator his own salt-scraper kept beneath his market counter — a short iron-bladed tool with a dark reddish-brown stain across the flat of the blade that he says is residue from a red-clay ceremonial salt he handles.

[char:Dauvek Pol] tells the investigator that his aunt had, in his view, been quietly adjusting the recorded salt-weights in the Tessek ledgers for years in her own favor, and says that if someone had finally found proof of it, that person would have had reason to act before she could destroy the evidence.
Shelves of bound ledgers and rolled documents line every wall, and the single lamp bracket above the reading table has left a ghost of soot on the plaster ceiling.

The investigator finds [item:Salt Ledger]. A thick bound ledger lying open to a page dense with columns of figures; the ink in the rightmost column of the open page is smeared in a broad streak, as though the page was disturbed before the ink had dried.

On the inner face of the [loc:records_room] doorframe, at roughly waist height, there is a rust-brown smear shaped like a partial hand-wipe — the heel of a palm pressed and then dragged downward across the wood.

The open page of the [loc:records_room] ledger carries a final entry dated [time:the night of the death], written in the same careful hand as the surrounding columns; the ink in the rightmost column is smeared in a broad streak across the figures, as if the page was sharply disturbed before the ink had finished drying.
The main hall smells of cedar oil and old salt, and the Tessek lineage marks above the door lintel have been repainted so many times the original lines are lost beneath them.

The investigator encounters [char:Nitha Tessek] at [loc:matrons_house]. The Matron of the Tessek lineage, a sharp-faced woman of sixty who carries her authority the way others carry grief — always present, always shaping how she moves through a room. She makes no secret of the fact that she and Oravi had clashed repeatedly over lineage records, and she speaks of the death with genuine shock and unconcealed relief.

[char:Nitha Tessek] tells the investigator that she herself locked the [loc:records_room] door the previous evening before retiring, and when she checked the following morning, the door stood unlocked and the interior bolt had been forced out of its bracket.

[char:Nitha Tessek] tells the investigator that she was in the corridor outside the [loc:records_room] late that same evening, and shows a tear along the hem of her ceremonial sash — the kind of snag, she says, that happens on the iron bracket beside the Records Room door.

[char:Nitha Tessek] tells the investigator that [char:Tessek Oravi] had blocked the re-registration of a Tessek lineage property on three separate occasions over the past decade, and that the dispute had grown into an open enmity between them that everyone in [loc:matrons_house] knew about.

The key fits the [loc:records_room] lock exactly; on the exterior face of the lock plate, there are three fresh scratches radiating from the keyhole — bright metal, not yet tarnished — consistent with a key inserted in poor light or in haste.

time_of_death.confirmedAnswer: Between the second bell of night and first light

submissions.submittedAnswer: Between the second bell of night and first light
